title: Houses for Rent in Cleveland Under $800
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Cleveland Under $800/mo
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Cleveland under $800 a month? See realistic rent ranges by bedroom size and where this price point exists in Cleveland.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent cleveland ohio under 800
answerBox: Houses for rent under $800 a month in Cleveland exist, but they are concentrated at the smaller end of the market — mostly 1- and 2-bedroom units, often duplexes rather than standalone houses. Citywide rents run roughly $700 to $1,800, with a typical rent near $1,000, so $800 sits below the midpoint.

sections.h2: Can you find a house for rent under $800 in Cleveland?
body: Yes, but be realistic about what that price point gets you: in our own Cleveland selection, rents range from about $700 to $1,800 a month with a typical rent near $1,000, and the lowest prices generally attach to 1-bedroom or smaller 2-bedroom units rather than full-size houses. A sub-$800 listing is more likely to be one unit of a duplex or a compact 2-bedroom than a large single-family home.

We work with rental homes across Cleveland's east and southeast side, and every home we work with accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us your budget and bedroom needs — we'll point you to whatever fits under $800 right now, or the closest realistic alternative.

sections.h2: What does $800 actually rent in Cleveland?
body: Zumper's July 2026 report put Cleveland's median studio rent at $1,060 and 1-bedroom median at $1,195 across all listings citywide — figures pulled largely from apartment inventory, which tends to run higher than smaller independent rental houses. Within our own selection, the lowest-priced available units (1-bedroom) start around $700, and 2-bedroom units start around $750, which is the realistic range where a sub-$800 budget lands.

sections.h2: Where in Cleveland has lower-cost rental housing?
body: Our lower-cost inventory is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, in neighborhoods including **Slavic Village (44105)**, **Collinwood (44110)**, **Glenville (44108)**, and **Fairfax/Central (44104)** — areas with a large stock of early-1900s duplexes and smaller single-family homes. We also manage a smaller number of homes on the west side (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway, Old Brooklyn) and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio.

sections.h2: Stretching a tight budget without cutting corners
body: If $800 is a hard ceiling, widen your search to 1-bedroom units and duplex units rather than standalone houses, and ask directly whether utilities are included — a lower base rent with tenant-paid heat can end up costing more in Cleveland's winter months than a slightly higher rent with utilities bundled in. Our [utilities-included houses page](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-cleveland-utilities-included/) explains what to ask about before you sign.

It also helps to compare unit sizes directly: our [2-bedroom houses page](/cleveland-rentals/houses/2-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/) and [under-$1,000 page](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio-under-1000/) cover the price bands just above this one, in case a slightly higher budget opens up meaningfully more inventory.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers and low-cost Cleveland rentals
body: If you're searching under $800 because you're using a Housing Choice Voucher, know that the voucher payment standard (not your rent alone) determines what a household can afford — check current figures with Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) at [cmha.net](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Every home we work with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of price point.

faq.q: Are there houses for rent in Cleveland under $800 a month?
faq.a: Some exist, but mostly as 1-bedroom units or the smaller unit of a duplex rather than full standalone houses. Our own Cleveland rents start around $700, with the bulk of 2-bedroom homes running $750–$1,100. Book a showing and share your budget so we can point you to realistic current options.

faq.q: What's the cheapest type of rental in Cleveland?
faq.a: 1-bedroom units and smaller 2-bedroom duplex units tend to be the lowest-cost rental homes in Cleveland, generally starting around $700–$900 a month. Full 3-bedroom-plus houses rarely fall under $1,000 in our available homes.

faq.q: Do landlords in this price range accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Every home we work with, at any price point, accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Voucher holders should confirm their payment standard with CMHA before searching so they know the real budget ceiling their voucher supports.

faq.q: Which Cleveland neighborhoods have the most affordable rental houses?
faq.a: Our lower-cost inventory concentrates on Cleveland's east and southeast side — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, and Fairfax/Central — where the housing stock includes many smaller early-1900s duplexes and houses. We also manage a smaller number of west-side and Akron/Lorain/Elyria homes.
